justification: All pass criteria met: (1) req.user?.id optional chaining risk clearly identified with explanation that userId becomes undefined, (2) authorization before data access principle stated — fix says 'Guard early... before any DB call', (3) IDOR order enumeration risk explicitly called out as critical finding #2 with 404/403 differentiation, (4) Critical severity assigned and BLOCK verdict given, (5) safe_to_deploy is false. Additionally identified the undefined===undefined customerId bypass scenario, input validation gaps, and data exposure risks.

observations.text: Precisely identified the undefined===undefined bypass when order.customerId is also undefined — this is the key auth bypass the rubric targets
IDOR enumeration via 404/403 differentiation clearly explained with reproduction steps and correct fix (return 404 for both)
Correctly states fix should guard userId before any DB call, addressing authorization-before-data-access principle
Bonus finding on NoSQL/SQL injection via unsanitized orderId is valid and adds value
Clean reproduction steps for each finding make issues actionable
IDOR finding rated High rather than Critical — for a public-facing auth endpoint the rubric expects Critical classification, though overall verdict is still BLOCK
